candidate_problems.text: What exactly is the selection criterion that distinguishes genuine semantic equilibrium from merely locally useful coordination? Repeated success can stabilize arbitrary conventions, deceptive equilibria, or self-sealing inferential loops, so the conjecture needs a sharper account of when stability tracks meaning rather than just path-dependent social lock-in.

How does this equilibrium view connect symbols to anything beyond the game itself? If meaning is constituted by durable inferential and coordinative role, an open problem is whether and how the framework explains reference to mind-independent reality rather than only inter-agent coherence, especially for cases where a community can coordinate successfully while being systematically wrong.

What are the minimal structural conditions under which iterated interpretive games actually produce stable concepts? It remains open which variables are necessary or sufficient—memory, shared feedback, observability of error, agent heterogeneity, incentives, embodiment, institutional enforcement—and whether there are phase transitions where meaning emerges, fragments, or drifts.
